mnemonics.title: OS SERVICES MEMORIZATION
mnemonics.content: PICK-FIS: PROCESS MANAGEMENT, I/O MANAGEMENT, COMMUNICATION, KERNEL SERVICES, FILE MANAGEMENT, INFORMATION MAINTENANCE, SECURITY. This helps remember the major OS service categories.

mnemonics.title: MEMORY MANAGEMENT FUNCTIONS
mnemonics.content: DAM AC: DEFINE, ALLOCATE, MOVE, ACCESS, COMPACT. These are key memory management operations.

mnemonics.title: PROCESS MANAGEMENT OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: CREATE-SCHEDULE-TERMINATE: C-S-T. The three main operations in process management.

acronyms.usage: The fundamental software that manages hardware and provides services to users and applications.
Mechanism for processes to communicate and synchronize their actions.
Text-based user interface where users type commands to interact with the OS.
Visual interface using windows, icons, and menus for user interaction.
Security mechanism that defines who can access specific resources.
The primary hardware component that executes instructions, managed by OS process scheduling.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a state diagram showing NEW, READY, RUNNING, WAITING, and TERMINATED states with arrows showing transitions between them.
Create a pyramid diagram with registers at top (fastest), cache, main memory, and disk storage at bottom (slowest but largest).
Draw concentric circles with hardware at center, then kernel services, then OS services, then user applications at outer layer.
Show a three-stage buffer: CPU sends data to buffer, then to device, with arrows indicating flow in both directions.

analogies.analogy: Just as a government provides essential services (security, transportation, communication) to citizens, an OS provides services (process management, memory management, file access) to programs and users.
Like a factory manager schedules workers and tasks, OS process management schedules CPU time for different processes.

analogies.concept: Memory Management as Library Book Management
analogies.analogy: Like a librarian tracks which books are borrowed and available, memory management tracks which memory locations are allocated and free.
Just as a file cabinet organizes documents in folders and drawers, file management organizes data in files and directories.

analogies.concept: Security Service as Building Security Guard
analogies.analogy: Like a security guard checks identification and controls building access, OS security checks user credentials and controls resource access.
